--- Comment #2 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
I've fixed this in pure R (in a way it should be relatively fast in case there
are no `"Inf"` or `"-Inf"`) in svn r89500 on Feb.28 _and_ added a `TODO`
commenting 
```
## TODO efficiently: in C's in strptime_internal() in ../../../main/Rstrptime.h
```

but close this for now, as the bug is fixed, and the `TODO` does not seem
urgent to me.

text:
*[Bug 19006] as.POSIX{ct,lt}(<character>) fails for "Inf"*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19006>

--- Comment #3 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Martin Maechler from comment #2)
`match(c("-Inf","Inf"), x, 0L)` is not so fast.

With `[<-.POSIXlt` that match time zone to the subassignment target, I think it
is better to supply `POSIXlt` object with the same time zone, or to supply
non-`POSIXlt` object and let `[<-.POSIXlt` do the conversion.

For index for repeated usage, like in `[<-.POSIXlt`, numeric index is more
efficient.

    if(length(i <- which(x == "-Inf"))) r[i] <- -Inf
    if(length(i <- which(x ==  "Inf"))) r[i] <-  Inf

can be used.

text:
*[Bug 19015] New: R segmentation fault in Windows with empty -e argument*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19015>

            Bug ID: 19015
           Summary: R segmentation fault in Windows with empty -e argument
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Windows 64-bit
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Windows GUI / Window specific
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:bill.r-project@denney.ws|bill.r-project@denney.ws>

R version: R 4.5.2 (2025-10-31 ucrt)
Platform: x86_64-w64-mingw32/x64, Windows 11 (build 26200)
Not reproducible on: R 4.5.2 on Linux 6.14.0 x86_64

Summary
On Windows, passing an empty string to Rscript -e "" causes a crash
(STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION, exit code -1073741819 / 0xC0000005). This affects
Rscript.exe, R.exe, and Rterm.exe. On Linux, the same command produces a clean
error message and exits with code 1.

This is easily triggered in practice when using Git Bash (MSYS2/MinGW), because
multi-line -e arguments (common in shell scripts) have their newlines treated
as argument separators. When a -e argument begins with a newline, the first
piece becomes an empty string, triggering the crash.

Minimal reproducible examples
From any Windows shell (cmd.exe, PowerShell, Git Bash):

Rscript -e ""

Exit code: -1073741819 (0xC0000005 STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION)

All three executables crash:

Rscript -e ""
R --no-echo -e ""
Rterm --no-echo -e ""

From Git Bash / MSYS2 (additional trigger via newline splitting):

# Crashes — leading newline produces empty first piece
Rscript -e $'\ncat(1)'

# Only prints "1", cat(2) silently discarded — newline splits the argument
Rscript -e $'cat(1)\ncat(2)'

Note: the newline-splitting behavior is a Git Bash / MSYS2 argument-passing
characteristic, not an R bug per se. But it makes the empty-expression crash
easy to hit in practice when writing multi-line -e scripts.

Expected behavior
Rscript -e "" should produce a clean error message (as it does on Linux:
"option '-e' requires a non-empty argument") and exit with a non-zero code, not
crash.

This does not occur in Linux.
